purpose: M(fit-1) acceptance oracle: first qpAdm GLS fit (CpuBackend, FP64, one model) must match these to bit/tolerance parity per architecture.md S12 PARITY LAW + S13.

canonical_invocation: qpadm(f2_dir, left=left, right=right, target=target, boot=FALSE, getcov=TRUE, return_f4=TRUE, fudge=1e-4, cpp=TRUE)  with set.seed(42)
note: Values reproduce BIT-EXACT (all 15 digits) when qpadm is given the f2 DIRECTORY PATH directly. Calling read_f2(dir) then qpadm(f2_blocks,...) with non-default read args yields a ~1e-5 difference (different internal f2-read arg set); the directory-path form is canonical. The committed binary fixture fixtures/f2_fit0_9pop.bin is the read_f2(dir) f2-OBJECT tensor; the fixture-path values (what test_qpadm_parity reproduces) are recorded under fixture_f2_object_path.
source_correction: REGENERATED from the convertf-converted PACKEDANCESTRYMAP (convertf v8621: v66 TGENO -> PA at /workspace/data/aadr/converted_pa/v66_HO_pa). admixtools 2.0.10 does NOT support the raw v66 .geno TGENO format and silently misread it, producing the prior CORRUPT golden (500848 SNPs, weights [0.559,0.441]). AT2 reads the convertf-PA correctly: 391333 SNPs after filtering / 351539 polymorphic / 710 jackknife blocks, weights [CordedWare 0.8688, Turkey_N 0.1312] -- matching steppe's own decode (391333 SNPs, weights ~[0.866,0.134]).

source_geno_format: PACKEDANCESTRYMAP (convertf v8621 from the raw v66 TGENO)

description: qpadm internal f4 matrix flattened: row left[-1]=Turkey_N over the 5 non-ref rights (rightref=Mbuti). OQ-1/OQ-3 seam golden. (directory-path / read_f2(dir) values.)
5x5 jackknife covariance, UNfudged (qpadm fudges diag by += fudge*tr(Q) before inverting). (directory-path / read_f2(dir) values.)

The f2-OBJECT-path qpadm result on the committed binary fixture fixtures/f2_fit0_9pop.bin (read_f2(dir) tensor, P=9, nb=710). These are the values test_qpadm_parity.cu reproduces (it loads the fixture, NOT the directory). They differ from the directory-path golden above by the documented ~1e-5 read-arg caveat.
